rationale: The citing editorial references Tavassoli and Lee (2003) multiple times in its core theoretical discussion of why modality matters, not just in a generic literature list. It uses the paper as evidence that multimodal combinations can create both synergies and interference and that some modality combinations can be more detrimental to cognitive processing than others. The citation also appears as a representative multimodal study in the paper's organizing table. Together, these uses materially inform the conceptual framing of the article.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Tavassoli and Lee (2003) as prior empirical grounding for key claims about auditory-visual interaction effects in consumer processing. This serves as part of the conceptual foundation for the editorial's media-versus-modality framework in multimedia marketing research.
